style_prefix: Pen-and-watercolor illustration in the style of a contemporary illustrated ski guidebook. Confident black ink contour lines drawn with a fine pen describe every form, then filled with richly saturated watercolor washes — dense, layered, with visible brushstrokes and patches of unpainted white paper showing through within shapes. Visible cross-hatching and stippling in shadows. Crisp edges where ink meets paint. Detailed and storybook-illustrated, not loose or impressionistic. Palette: full-color but rooted in alpine blues, warm earth browns, deep evergreens, fresh snow whites, with a single saturated red accent where the subject calls for it. Center the subject in the frame; allow a thin white deckled paper border around the entire image. Do not exceed the borders of a single panel.

slots.prompt: Full-bleed opening watercolor: describe the signature scene — who, where, what they're wearing, what's happening, what's behind them.
Signature object for this resort — one iconic vehicle / lift / structure painted on cream paper ground.
Mid-mountain portrait scene of the daughter, distant skiers/conifers behind. Describe outfit and setting.
Character portrait, head-and-shoulders: [name + diagnostic details]. Plain cream background.

Atmospheric watercolor panorama of this resort's valley at dusk or dawn — soft colors, mountain silhouettes, faint village lights, calm empty lower band intended for small text overlay. No text, no labels.

Hand-painted antique-style aerial map, 3/4 perspective. Describe the peaks, terrain, lower-mountain features, village location. No text, no pins, no route lines — those are overlaid digitally afterward. Just the painted base map.
